articles.headline: Fourth World Cup still has South Korea captain Son Heung-Min feeling 'like a kid'
articles.description: South Korea captain Son Heung-min says he feels like a "kid" as he enters his fourth World Cup, where he will debut against the Czech Republic in a Group A match in Guadalajara on Thursday.

images.name: Son Heung-Min [1296x729]
images.caption: Son Heung-Min was all smiles on Wednesday at South Korea's final training session before their 2026 FIFA World Cup opener against Czechia.
images.credit: Ulises RUIZ / AFP via Getty Images

articles.headline: Will 2026 prove to be Son Heung-Min's final FIFA World Cup hurrah?
articles.description: Son Heung-Min's previous three World Cups perfectly mirrored where he was in his career. So what does 2026 hold for him?

images.name: Will the 2026 World Cup be Son Heung-Min's last hurrah? [1500x844]
Son Heung-Min 2014 World Cup [1296x729]
images.caption: Son Heung-Min's first World Cup goal came in South Korea's ill-fated 2014 campaign -- where they picked up just one point from three games in their worst showing since 1998.
images.credit: ADRIAN DENNIS / AFP via Getty Images

images.name: Son Heung-Min 2018 World Cup [1296x729]
images.caption: Son Heung-Min's memorable sealer in a stunning 2-0 win over Germany in 2018 ultimately proved in vain as South Korea narrowly failed to make it out of the group stage.

images.name: Son Heung-Min 2022 World Cup [1296x729]
images.caption: South Korea finally reached the round of 16 at the 2022 FIFA World Cup for the first time in 12 years -- although that was where their campaign would end with a 4-1 loss to Brazil.

images.name: Will Mexico or South Korea top Group A at the World Cup?
images.caption: Gab & Juls debate who will win Group A between Mexico, South Korea, Czechia and South Africa at the FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Japan have a plan to win the World Cup by 2050. How close will they get in 2026?
articles.description: Japan's "100 Year Vision" is well ahead of schedule, but are the Samurai Blue ready win the World Cup now?

images.name: Japan World Cup chances [1500x844]
images.alt: Wataru Endo, Takefusa Kubo and Ritsu Doan

images.name: Wataru Endo [1296x729]
images.caption: Although he was sidelined by injury in February, Wataru Endo has recovered in time for this third FIFA World Cup -- and first as captain of Japan.

images.name: Japan vs. Brazil [1296x729]
images.caption: Japan's preparations for the 2026 FIFA World Cup saw them claim friendly victories over Brazil and England for the first time ever.

images.name: Have injuries ruined Japan's chances of causing World Cup upsets?
images.caption: Gabe Tan reacts to Japan's squad for the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: World Cup final squads ranked: Of all 48 national teams, who can win this summer?
articles.description: We have the final 26-player squads that can win the FIFA World Cup, so let's go through all 48 teams and rank where they stand.

images.name: Pochettino: USMNT are moving in a good direction ahead of World Cup
images.caption: Mauricio Pochettino speaks after the USMNT's 3-2 win over Senegal ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

categories.description: FIFA World Cup Qualifying - AFC/Concacaf Playoff

articles.headline: Midfield metronome Hwang In-Beom could be key for South Korea at the 2026 FIFA World Cup
articles.description: Hwang In-Beom is not South Korea's most-prominent name. But he could be their most important player at the World Cup.

images.name: Hwang In-Beom [1296x729]
images.caption: Although he was initially a doubt after picking up an injury in March, Hwang In-Beom has been passed fit to feature for South Korea at the 2026 FIFA World Cup -- where he will likely play a key role for them in midfield.

images.name: Hwang In-Beom [1296x729]
images.caption: Having had plenty of previous experience in Europe with Rubin Kazan, Olympiacos and Red Star Belgrade, Hwang In-Beom has now spent the past two seasons in the Eredivisie with Feyenoord.

images.name: Son Heung-min set for fourth World Cup with South Korea
images.caption: South Korea name their 26-man squad for the 2026 World Cup, headlined by a fourth competition appearance for captain Son Heung-min.

articles.headline: Why Japan could be the most exciting team at the 2026 FIFA World Cup
articles.description: Even with a couple of key players out through injury, Japan could still be the most exciting team at the 2026 World Cup.

images.name: Japan [1296x729]
images.caption: Japan's preparation for the 2026 FIFA World Cup has seen them record victories over Brazil and England for the first time ever over the past seven months.

images.name: Kaoru Mitoma [1296x729]
images.caption: Japan were dealt a real blow ahead of the World Cup after injuy ruled out Kaoru Mitoma, who scored the only goal of the game when they beat England 1-0 at Wembley back in March.

images.name: Have injuries ruined Japan's chances of causing World Cup upsets?
images.caption: Gabe Tan reacts to Japan's squad for the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

season.name: 2026 World Cup Qualifying - AFC, Second Round

article.headline: Socceroos thrash Bangladesh 7-0 in first WC qualifier
article.description: The Socceroos have kicked off their World Cup qualifying campaign in scintillating style, with Jamie Maclaren scoring a hat-trick in a 7-0 thumping of Bangladesh.
article.linkText: Socceroos thrash Bangladesh 7-0 in first WC qualifier

images.name: Jamie Maclaren [1296x518]
images.caption: Jamie Maclaren celebrating a goal against Bangladesh.

peers.name: Jamie Maclaren [1296x1296]
peers.caption: Jamie Maclaren celebrating a goal against Bangladesh.

peers.name: Jamie Maclaren [1296x729]
peers.caption: Jamie Maclaren celebrating a goal against Bangladesh.

peers.name: Jamie Maclaren [864x1296]
peers.caption: Jamie Maclaren celebrating a goal against Bangladesh.

peers.name: Jamie Maclaren [1296x864]
peers.caption: Jamie Maclaren celebrating a goal against Bangladesh.

peers.name: Jamie Maclaren [1296x518]
peers.caption: Jamie Maclaren celebrating a goal against Bangladesh.

images.name: Socceroos thrash Bangladesh to kick off WCQ campaign
images.caption: The Australian men's national team started their 2026 World Cup qualification campaign strongly, netting seven goals against Bangladesh with no return.

article.story:
The Socceroos have kicked off their World Cup qualifying campaign in scintillating style, with Jamie Maclaren scoring a hat-trick in a 7-0 thumping of Bangladesh.

In Graham Arnold's record-breaking 59th 'A international' as head coach, Australia made light work of the world No.183 in front of 20,876 fans at AAMI Park.

Harry Souttar opened the scoring in just the fourth minute with Brandon Borrello adding a second in the 20th.

Mitch Duke earned a rapid-fire brace in the 37th and 40th minutes while Melbourne City striker Maclaren came off the bench to score in the 48th, 70th and 84th minutes.

Australia next head to Kuwait to play Palestine on Tuesday night.

With Martin Boyle (knee) sidelined, Arnold opted to start Craig Goodwin and Borrello on the wings, with Duke up top.

Australia had 29 shots to Bangladesh's one and earned their first goal after Craig Goodwin was cut down.

Goodwin took the free kick, curling a tantalising left-footed ball towards Souttar.

The 198cm defender was completely untroubled as he rose highest to head into the top corner, notching his ninth international goal.

For Australia's second, the brilliant Connor Metcalfe found Lewis Miller and burst forward to meet the full-back's clever dink.

Metcalfe rode a challenge then brilliantly squared it to Borrello to tap home.

Australia cracked Bangladesh's defence a third time when Metcalfe dinked the ball towards Duke, who flicked home a header.

Three minutes later, Borrello fizzed a fierce strike off the post but Duke buried the rebound.

The 4-0 half-time lead was the Socceroos' highest-scoring first half since putting five goals past Taiwan in December 2012.

Goodwin, Jackson Irvine and Duke made way for Aziz Behich, Massimo Luongo and Maclaren at half-time, with Jordan Bos pushed to the wing.

Minutes later, Bos teed up Maclaren to tap home Australia's fifth.

Maclaren appeared offside but there was no VAR to rule it out.

Australia added a sixth when Luongo was denied but Maclaren coolly buried the follow-up.

Striker Kusini Yengi debuted for Australia in the 72nd minute.

Maclaren completed his second international hat-trick when he turned home Miller's excellent cross.

Luongo had a poor penalty saved in the 90th minute after Aiden O'Neill was brought down, leaving the Socceroos to settle for seven goals.

The game was a 50-year celebration of the 1973 Socceroos, the first to qualify for a World Cup.

It was also 18 years to the day since Australia beat Uruguay on penalties to reach the 2006 tournament.

keyEvents.text: Goal!  Australia 1, Bangladesh 0. Harry Souttar (Australia) header from the centre of the box to the top left corner. Assisted by Craig Goodwin with a cross   following a set piece situation.
Rakib Hossain (Bangladesh) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal!  Australia 2, Bangladesh 0. Brandon Borrello (Australia) right footed shot from very close range to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Connor Metcalfe.
Goal!  Australia 3, Bangladesh 0. Mitch Duke (Australia) header from the centre of the box to the top left corner. Assisted by Connor Metcalfe with a cross.
Goal!  Australia 4, Bangladesh 0. Mitch Duke (Australia) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom right corner.
Second Half begins Australia 4, Bangladesh 0.
Substitution, Australia. Jamie Maclaren replaces Mitch Duke.
Substitution, Australia. Aziz Behich replaces Craig Goodwin.
Substitution, Australia. Massimo Luongo replaces Jackson Irvine.
Goal!  Australia 5, Bangladesh 0. Jamie Maclaren (Australia) right footed shot from very close range to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Jordy Bos with a cross.
Hasan Murad (Bangladesh) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Keanu Baccus (Australia) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Bangladesh. Shakil Hossain replaces Hasan Murad.
Substitution, Bangladesh. Robiul Hasan replaces Jamal Bhuyan.
Substitution, Bangladesh. Rafiqul Islam replaces Rakib Hossain.
Substitution, Australia. Aiden O'Neill replaces Keanu Baccus.
Goal!  Australia 6, Bangladesh 0. Jamie Maclaren (Australia) right footed shot from very close range to the bottom right corner.
Substitution, Bangladesh. Shekh Morsalin replaces Foysal Ahmed Fahim.
Substitution, Australia. Kusini Yengi replaces Brandon Borrello.
Saad Uddin (Bangladesh) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal!  Australia 7, Bangladesh 0. Jamie Maclaren (Australia) right footed shot from very close range to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Lewis Miller with a cross.
Substitution, Bangladesh. Rahmat Mia replaces Bishwanath Ghosh.
Penalty saved. Massimo Luongo (Australia)  right footed shot saved  in the bottom left corner.
